critic_raw: The aggressive oversampling of 410 defect images to create many synthetic positives raises a data contamination concern. If near-duplicate synthetic images end up in validation or test splits, the model's measured performance is inflated relative to its performance on genuinely new defect images. The nested cross-validation setup adds complexity — it is not always clear in nested CV implementations whether the augmentation policy is correctly isolated to inner training folds. Additionally, the description of 'part-level clustering' suggests that the check was done on original images, but augmented versions of those images may have visual characteristics similar to images from the same or nearby physical parts that were assigned to different folds.
defender_raw: The nested cross-validation performs splits at the original-image level before augmentation. Augmentations — rotations, flips, brightness changes — are applied on the fly inside training folds. No augmented image ever exists in the validation or test data because augmentation is fold-local and applied only during training. The part-level clustering check prevents the original images from crossing boundaries. Since augmentation happens after fold assignment and only in training, augmented near-duplicates of validation images cannot appear in the training fold by the pipeline's construction. The later-week production test set uses only raw images from a period after the development data, providing additional temporal separation. The claim is correctly scoped to this camera and process.
adjudication_raw: The critic's concern is structurally valid but does not apply to the described pipeline. The defender correctly explains why: augmentation is fold-local by construction, the split occurs before augmentation, and the final test uses raw later-week data. The part-level clustering addresses near-duplicate contamination at the original-image level, which is the only level at which the split operates. Defense wins.
all_issues_raised: augmentation leakage in nested CV — resolved by fold-local augmentation policy
part-level clustering scope concern — resolved by split-before-augmentation design
